title: Vajrapoha Falls
tagline: Vajrapoha Falls — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Vajrapoha Falls (also Vajrapoya waterfalls) is a waterfall in the Belgaum district of Karnataka in India.
overview.description: Vajrapoha Falls (also Vajrapoya waterfalls) is a waterfall in the Belgaum district of Karnataka in India. It is situated in a mountainous forest 8.5 kilometres (5.3 mi) in south west direction from the village of Jamboti. Between the village of Gavali and Chapoli on an elevated hillock, the Mandovi River that flows to the beautiful Vajrapoha Falls that fall for up to 200 metres (660 ft) and are best seen after the monsoon season (June–October). The falls are about 1.5 hours southwest of Belgaum.

topPlaces.name: Pastoli
topPlaces.description: Pastoli is a village in Belgaum district of Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Chapoli
topPlaces.description: Chapoli is a village in Belgaum district in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located amid the thick and dense Western Ghats forest which provides habitat for leopards, bears and other wild animals.

topPlaces.name: Amagaon
topPlaces.description: Amagaon is a village in Belagavi district bordered by Goa in west in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Amagaon nestles in the dense forests of the Western Ghats in and is known for its heavy rainfall.

topPlaces.name: Jamboti
topPlaces.description: Jamboti (Konkani: Zambotim) is an area located 28 kilometres (17 mi) south-west of Belgaum and 18 kilometres (11 mi) west of Khanapur in the Belgaum District of Karnataka, India. Mandovi River, the longest river in Goa, has its origin in the Jamboti hills.

topPlaces.name: Bhimgad Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Bhimgad Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in the Western Ghats, in Khanapur Taluk of Belgavi District near Jamboti Village, Karnataka state, India. This 19,042.58 ha (73.5238 mi2) of Tropical and subtropical moist broadleaf forests forest area was long awaiting to be a wild life sanctuary and finally declared in December 2011.

topPlaces.name: Amate (village)
topPlaces.description: Amate is a village in the Khanapur taluk of the Belagavi district, Karnataka, India. It has a total area of about 14.27 square kilometers.

topPlaces.name: Betne
topPlaces.description: Betne is a village in Belgaum district in the southern state of Karnataka, India.

This 19,042.58 ha (73.5238 mi2) of Tropical and subtropical moist broadleaf forests forest area was long awaiting to be a wild life sanctuary and finally declared in December 2011.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vajrapoha Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vajrapoha Falls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vajrapoha Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Belagavi Airport (40 km) and the nearest railway station is Madgaon Junction (Goa) (56 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vajrapoha Falls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vajrapoha Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vajrapoha Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Vajrapoha Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vajrapoha Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pastoli, Chapoli, Amagaon, Jamboti, Bhimgad Wildlife Sanctuary.
